rules.cancer_doctors: Only named doctors/clinics with cancer patient treatment evidence or cancer clinic status. 10 requires professional doctor/clinic plus Kruse-protocol cancer evidence.
rules.other_practitioners: Professional/research/clinic-adjacent leads that are related but not proven cancer+Kruse treatment providers.
rules.specific_people: Forum handles or people who may help, separated from professional doctor/clinic sheet.

cancerDoctors.Name: Kruse Longevity Center / Dr. Jack Kruse
cancerDoctors.Kruse Protocols Evidenced: AM sun/UV/IR; grounding; low nnEMF/low tech; DDW/Somlyai; seafood/DHA; CT when tolerated; circadian timing; tropical/caldera relocation for severe cases
cancerDoctors.Cancer Treatment Details: Directly advised pancreatic cancer, GBM/glioma, breast cancer, and cervical cancer cases. Guidance is environmental/mitochondrial: change the entire light-water-magnetism environment, use DDW intelligently, avoid blue/nnEMF, and time unavoidable conventional therapy circadianly.
cancerDoctors.Source Evidence: Pancreatic cancer: 'change everything' and read Gabor/Somlyai. Breast cancer: DDW/Somlyai plus sun/grounding/CT/low-tech stack. GBM: tropical/caldera sunlight context. Cervical cancer: redox-first stack, while drug protocol questions deferred to Makis.
cancerDoctors.Reasoning: 10 because he is the source of the Kruse cancer framework and gives direct cancer-case guidance. Not an oncologist row; this is a Kruse-protocol authority row.
cancerDoctors.Review / Next Step: Use as protocol authority; for conventional oncology decisions use treating oncologist/surgeon.

cancerDoctors.Name: Dr. John Gordon
cancerDoctors.Kruse Protocols Evidenced: Kruse-aligned mitochondrial surgeon; case used DDW/Somlyai, sun/UV, grounding, seafood/DHA, CT, low tech, no light after sunset, methylene blue, iodine
cancerDoctors.Cancer Treatment Details: Breast cancer patient Vervaina traveled to NOLA/Metairie to see him. Gordon assessed tumor/surgery timing, warned survival odds were poor without chemo, and advised finding an oncologist; Jack explicitly vouched for him.
cancerDoctors.Source Evidence: Patient: came to NOLA to see Dr John Gordon. Jack: 'I trust John huge' and called him a serious mitochondrial/Black Swan surgeon. Jack said his ideas would be powerful in Gordon's hands.
cancerDoctors.Reasoning: 10 because this is a real named doctor directly involved in an active cancer case and specifically endorsed by Jack inside a Kruse-protocol context.
cancerDoctors.Review / Next Step: High-priority lead. Verify current practice/contact and cancer case scope.

cancerDoctors.Name: Block Center / Keith Block, MD
cancerDoctors.Kruse Protocols Evidenced: Circadian / chrono-chemotherapy; possible grounding/outdoor strategy during infusion discussed by patient
cancerDoctors.Cancer Treatment Details: Jack pointed Vervaina toward Keith Block as the US cancer MD doing chrono-chemotherapy. Patient started intake with Block Center after that pointer.
cancerDoctors.Source Evidence: Jack: Keith Block has been doing chrono-chemotherapy for years and is the cancer MD in the US for that approach. Patient: called Block Center and began intake.
cancerDoctors.Reasoning: 9 because it is a true cancer clinic with circadian chemotherapy evidence from Jack. Not 10 because source does not show full Kruse stack: DDW, nnEMF, sun/grounding, CT.
cancerDoctors.Review / Next Step: Strong clinic lead for patients who must do chemo and want circadian timing.

cancerDoctors.Name: Dr. William Makis
cancerDoctors.Kruse Protocols Evidenced: Used alongside Kruse stack: sun, DDW, grounding, seafood, methylene blue, PBM/sun, sweating, low tech; Makis protocol itself was ivermectin/mebendazole/supplements
cancerDoctors.Cancer Treatment Details: Catherine paid for a 3-month Makis service and followed his cervical cancer protocol with ivermectin and mebendazole. Jack deferred drug protocol questions to Makis.
cancerDoctors.Source Evidence: Catherine: paid Dr Makis for 3 months, received protocol, followed cervical cancer protocol with Iver and Mebendazole. Jack: 'Talk to Makis not me.'
cancerDoctors.Reasoning: 8 because he is a real cancer-protocol doctor in a forum case, but the Kruse environmental protocol appears to come from Jack/Catherine, not necessarily Makis.
cancerDoctors.Review / Next Step: Good drug-protocol lead; verify exact cancer specialty and current service.

cancerDoctors.Name: Dr. Zsofia Clemens / Paleomedicina
cancerDoctors.Kruse Protocols Evidenced: Strict Paleolithic Ketogenic Diet / PKD; metabolic cancer logic
cancerDoctors.Cancer Treatment Details: Forum members recommend Clemens/Paleomedicina for GBM/glioma and pancreatic cancer contexts. Protocol is strict PKD; not primarily DDW/light/EMF.
cancerDoctors.Source Evidence: GBM thread: Clemens deals with GBMs through strict PKD. Pancreatic cancer thread: member says somebody really good for cancer is Zsofia Clemens in Romania and links Paleomedicina.
cancerDoctors.Reasoning: 8 because it is a real cancer-related clinic/doctor lead, but the evidence is PKD/metabolic, not full Kruse DDW-light-EMF protocol.
cancerDoctors.Review / Next Step: Strong for GBM/PKD research; verify current intake and cancer types treated.

cancerDoctors.Name: Gerson Clinic / Gerson Therapy
cancerDoctors.Treats Cancer Patients?: Yes - cancer therapy lead, not Kruse-specific
cancerDoctors.Kruse Protocols Evidenced: Circadian sun missing-link discussion appears in forum, but not shown as part of clinic protocol
cancerDoctors.Cancer Treatment Details: Forum patient with brain surgery/tumor history went to Mexico for Gerson Therapy, stayed on protocol for two years, and later reconnected with Gerson clinic after seizure.
cancerDoctors.Source Evidence: Patient: went to Mexico for 3 weeks learning Gerson Therapy; later back in touch with Gerson clinic and described prior care as 'everything I'd hoped.'
cancerDoctors.Reasoning: 5 because it is a real cancer clinic/therapy lead, but the corpus does not show DDW, nnEMF, sunrise, grounding, or Kruse cancer protocol use by the clinic.
cancerDoctors.Review / Next Step: Use as historical/alternative cancer clinic lead, not a Kruse-protocol lead.

cancerDoctors.Name: Puna Wai Ora Mind-Body Cancer Clinic
cancerDoctors.Kruse Protocols Evidenced: Budwig oil/protein mixture; omega-3 emphasis. No DDW/light/EMF evidence in source.
cancerDoctors.Cancer Treatment Details: Mentioned as source for Budwig-style oil/protein mixture material.
cancerDoctors.Source Evidence: Post cites 'From: Puna Wai Ora Mind-Body Cancer Clinic' and alternative-cancer-care.com in a Budwig/oil-protein context.
cancerDoctors.Reasoning: 4 because it is a cancer clinic mention but not proven Kruse-aligned.
cancerDoctors.Review / Next Step: Low-priority unless specifically researching Budwig-style clinics.

cancerDoctors.Name: Dr. Nicholas Gonzalez
cancerDoctors.Kruse Protocols Evidenced: Gerson-like nutritional/enzyme therapy; EMF caveat mentioned by forum poster
cancerDoctors.Cancer Treatment Details: Mentioned as the more up-to-date version of Gerson; not a current clinic lead in the extracted forum data.
cancerDoctors.Source Evidence: Forum post: Nicholas Gonzalez MD is the most up-to-date version of Gerson; harder now with EMFs.
cancerDoctors.Reasoning: 4 because it is cancer-protocol relevant but historical/reference-only and not demonstrated as current Kruse care.
cancerDoctors.Review / Next Step: Reference lead, not primary outreach lead.

otherPractitioners.Name: Gabor Somlyai / HYD DDW framework
otherPractitioners.Why Related: Core DDW cancer framework repeatedly recommended in Kruse cancer threads.
otherPractitioners.Cancer / Protocol Details: Books and DDW approach cited for cancer, including pancreatic cancer and long-term DDW use. Practical forum discussions include ppm titration and duration.
otherPractitioners.Source Evidence: Jack told breast cancer patient to get Somlyai's book. Pancreatic cancer thread says Gabor's book has a lot on that tumor type.
otherPractitioners.Reasoning: 8: central DDW cancer source, but not a treating doctor/clinic row.
otherPractitioners.Review / Next Step: Use for DDW protocol logic; verify any current clinical service separately.

otherPractitioners.Name: Dr. Laszlo Boros
otherPractitioners.Why Related: DDW/deuterium expert; forum specifically mentions renal cancer/DDW work.
otherPractitioners.Cancer / Protocol Details: Forum says Boros has done notable work on renal cancers with DDW and discusses DDW slowing prostate/brain cancer. Another source says he is a biochemist, not a clinician.
otherPractitioners.Source Evidence: Cancer journey thread: amazing work on renal cancers with DDW; DDW thread: Boros is a biochemist and not a clinician.
otherPractitioners.Reasoning: 7: highly relevant DDW cancer science, but not a treating clinician in the forum evidence.
otherPractitioners.Review / Next Step: Use as DDW science lead, not first-line patient-care lead.

otherPractitioners.Name: Dr. Thomas Seyfried
otherPractitioners.Why Related: Metabolic cancer / ketogenic cancer framework overlaps with Kruse mitochondrial cancer view.
otherPractitioners.Cancer / Protocol Details: Cited for cancer as a metabolic disease, ketogenic/fasting/metabolic therapies, and Warburg/mitochondrial dysfunction.
otherPractitioners.Source Evidence: Forum quotes reviews recommending Seyfried's Cancer as a Metabolic Disease to cancer patients and specialists.
otherPractitioners.Reasoning: 6: cancer expertise is strong, but not a Kruse clinician/clinic and Jack critiques missing deuterium/biophysics.
otherPractitioners.Review / Next Step: Useful theory lead, not a Kruse cancer practitioner lead.

otherPractitioners.Name: Dr. Jerry Tennant
otherPractitioners.Why Related: Cancer electrical/voltage framing is adjacent to Kruse redox/electricity ideas.
otherPractitioners.Cancer / Protocol Details: Referenced for 'Healing is Voltage - Cancer's On/Off Switches: Polarity' and still practicing; source ties cancer to hypoxia/cellular voltage.
otherPractitioners.Source Evidence: Forum recommends Tennant to a liposarcoma patient and links Cancer's On/Off Switches material.
otherPractitioners.Reasoning: 5: real doctor and cancer-voltage relevance, but no evidence he treats cancer with Kruse DDW/sun/EMF protocols.
otherPractitioners.Review / Next Step: Secondary lead; verify current cancer-patient work.

otherPractitioners.Name: Dr. David Brownstein
otherPractitioners.Why Related: Iodine cancer anecdotes overlap with forum cancer protocols that mention Lugol's/iodine.
otherPractitioners.Cancer / Protocol Details: Referenced for iodine protocol and cancer anecdotes including uterine/vaginal and breast cancer context.
otherPractitioners.Source Evidence: Forum references Brownstein's iodine protocol and cancer cases.
otherPractitioners.Reasoning: 4: cancer-adjacent doctor; no source evidence of Kruse cancer protocol use.

otherPractitioners.Name: Dr. Jorge Flechas
otherPractitioners.Why Related: Iodine deficiency/cancer material is repeatedly cited.
otherPractitioners.Cancer / Protocol Details: Forum lists iodine deficiency and cancer resources and iodine sufficiency material.
otherPractitioners.Source Evidence: Jorge Flechas iodine deficiency & cancer and total body iodine sufficiency references.
otherPractitioners.Reasoning: 4: relevant to iodine, not proven Kruse cancer treatment.
otherPractitioners.Review / Next Step: Low-priority unless iodine is central to case.

otherPractitioners.Name: Dr. Dietrich Klinghardt
otherPractitioners.Why Related: RF/MW/EMF mitigation overlaps with Kruse nnEMF cancer environment logic.
otherPractitioners.Cancer / Protocol Details: Cited for RF/MW radiation warnings and biological protection; not presented as a cancer specialist in source.
otherPractitioners.Source Evidence: Forum: Klinghardt's specialty is Lyme/autism/infectious disease, and he has warned on RF/MW radiation for years.
otherPractitioners.Reasoning: 4: EMF-relevant practitioner, not cancer/DDW specialist in the forum evidence.
otherPractitioners.Review / Next Step: Useful for EMF/complex illness, not primary cancer lead.

otherPractitioners.Name: Dr. Paul Marik
otherPractitioners.Why Related: Mentioned as a doctor with broad medical knowledge and ivermectin-related protocol context.
otherPractitioners.Cancer / Protocol Details: Forum evidence found him in treatment/protocol context, not as a Kruse cancer practitioner.
otherPractitioners.Source Evidence: Post says to find doctors like Dr. Marik and lists his medical fields; other forum material references IVM.
otherPractitioners.Reasoning: 4: medically relevant, but no direct forum evidence of cancer patients treated with Kruse protocols.
otherPractitioners.Review / Next Step: Keep as adjacent protocol lead only.

specificPeople.Why May Help: Not a doctor row, but clinically relevant. Owns/operates an acupuncture clinic, joined Optimal Klub practitioners forum, sees patients, and reports DDW use with stage 4 cancer patients / cancer clinic next door.
specificPeople.Cancer / Protocol Details: Referred stage 4 cancer patients; began first patient on DDW 10ppm; works with cancer clinic next door that started three patients on 10ppm DDW and all three lowered cancer markers; Arizona/Phoenix/Hydro Health mentioned.
specificPeople.Source Evidence: AcuHealth: 'I am referred patients with stage 4 cancer...' and 'Work with Cancer clinic next door started last 3 patients 10ppm. All 3 lowered Cancer markers.'
specificPeople.Reasoning: 8 as a lead, not a doctor. Strong DDW/cancer patient evidence, but credentials/legal name/clinic identity are unknown.
specificPeople.Review / Next Step: Verify legal name, license, clinic, and whether cancer care is direct or through neighboring clinic.

specificPeople.Why May Help: Not a practitioner row. Useful because he repeatedly discusses DDW dosing, Somlyai/Boros material, and cancer-related DDW practicalities.
specificPeople.Cancer / Protocol Details: Discusses DDW-25/Qlarivia, Somlyai duration, ppm mixing, breath/saliva/urine deuterium testing, and DDW in cancer contexts.
specificPeople.Source Evidence: Multiple DDW threads; pancreatic cancer thread and Gordon cancer thread include practical DDW comments.
specificPeople.Reasoning: 6 as a knowledge lead only; not a doctor/clinic.
specificPeople.Review / Next Step: Use for forum navigation/DDW references, not medical guidance.

specificPeople.Why May Help: Not a practitioner row. Useful for finding GBM/PKD/Clemens/Somlyai references.
specificPeople.Cancer / Protocol Details: In GBM thread, points to Gabor Somlyai and Dr. Zsofia Clemens/Paleomedicina; notes PKD and missing light story.
specificPeople.Source Evidence: GBM thread: recommends Somlyai book and Clemens/Paleomedicina for GBM via strict PKD.
specificPeople.Reasoning: 5 as a useful navigator, not a practitioner.
specificPeople.Review / Next Step: Use to trace source links, not as clinical contact.
